name: Los Angeles
interest: Largest city in California and second-largest in the US; major economic, cultural, and entertainment hub

name: San Francisco
interest: Global tech and finance center on a hilly peninsula; iconic for fog, cable cars, and progressive politics

name: San Diego
interest: Major military and biotech hub near the Mexican border with year-round mild climate

name: Sacramento
interest: California state capital at the confluence of the Sacramento and American Rivers

name: San Jose
interest: Self-proclaimed capital of Silicon Valley and the most populous city in the Bay Area

name: Oakland
interest: Major West Coast port city with rich cultural diversity and growing tech presence

name: Fresno
interest: Agricultural powerhouse in the heart of the San Joaquin Valley; leading raisin and almond producer

name: Long Beach
interest: Home to one of the busiest container ports in the Western Hemisphere

name: Anaheim
interest: Home to Disneyland Resort and Angel Stadium; major tourism economy

name: Santa Ana
interest: Orange County seat with the highest population density in the county; predominantly Latino community

name: Riverside
interest: Birthplace of California's citrus industry; home to the historic Mission Inn

name: Stockton
interest: Inland port city connected to SF Bay via deep-water channel; faced historic bankruptcy in 2012

name: Irvine
interest: Master-planned city consistently ranked among America's safest; major tech and biomedical corridor

name: Chula Vista
interest: Second-largest city in the San Diego metro; US Olympic Training Center location

name: Fremont
interest: Home to Tesla's primary manufacturing plant and historic Niles Canyon railway

name: San Bernardino
interest: Gateway to the San Bernardino Mountains; site of the first McDonald's restaurant

name: Modesto
interest: Inspired the film American Graffiti; major food-processing center in the San Joaquin Valley

name: Fontana
interest: Home to Auto Club Speedway; former Kaiser Steel mill site now redeveloped

name: Moreno Valley
interest: One of California's fastest-growing cities; massive warehouse and logistics hub

name: Glendale
interest: Largest Armenian diaspora community in the US; home to DreamWorks Animation studios

name: Huntington Beach
interest: Surf City USA with 9.5 miles of uninterrupted beach; hosts the US Open of Surfing

name: Santa Clarita
interest: Third-largest city in LA County; major film and TV production location

name: Garden Grove
interest: Home to a thriving Vietnamese American community and the Crystal Cathedral

name: Oceanside
interest: Beach city adjacent to Camp Pendleton Marine base; historic wooden pier and surf culture

name: Bakersfield
interest: Heart of California's oil industry and birthplace of the Bakersfield Sound in country music

name: Yosemite National Park
interest: Iconic granite cliffs, waterfalls, and giant sequoias; UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1984

name: Joshua Tree National Park
interest: Where the Mojave and Colorado deserts meet; famous for unique Joshua trees and rock formations

name: Death Valley National Park
interest: Hottest place on Earth (134F record); lowest point in North America at Badwater Basin (-282 ft)

name: Redwood National Park
interest: Home to the tallest trees on Earth; old-growth coastal redwood forests along the Pacific

name: Point Reyes National Seashore
interest: Dramatic coastal headlands on its own tectonic plate; rich wildlife including tule elk

name: Big Sur
interest: 90 miles of rugged coastline along Highway 1; one of the most scenic drives in the world

name: Sequoia National Park
interest: Home to General Sherman, the largest tree by volume on Earth; established 1890

name: Kings Canyon National Park
interest: Contains one of the deepest canyons in North America; adjacent to Sequoia NP

name: Channel Islands National Park
interest: Five remote islands called the Galapagos of North America; 150+ endemic species

name: Lassen Volcanic National Park
interest: All four types of volcanoes found here; Lassen Peak last erupted 1914-1917

name: Pinnacles National Park
interest: Remnants of an ancient volcano split by the San Andreas Fault; California condor release site

name: Anza-Borrego Desert State Park
interest: Largest state park in California; spectacular spring wildflower blooms in wet years

name: Mt Tamalpais State Park
interest: Birthplace of mountain biking; sweeping views of the Bay Area and Pacific Ocean

name: Muir Woods National Monument
interest: Old-growth coastal redwoods just 12 miles north of San Francisco; named for John Muir

name: Humboldt Redwoods State Park
interest: Avenue of the Giants; contains the largest remaining contiguous old-growth redwood forest

name: Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park
interest: Home to McWay Falls, an 80-foot waterfall dropping directly onto a beach cove

name: McArthur-Burney Falls Memorial State Park
interest: 129-foot waterfall Teddy Roosevelt called the eighth wonder of the world

name: Calaveras Big Trees State Park
interest: First giant sequoia grove discovered by Euro-Americans in 1852; two magnificent groves

name: Henry Cowell Redwoods State Park
interest: 40-acre old-growth redwood grove near Santa Cruz; popular Roaring Camp Railway

name: Prairie Creek Redwoods State Park
interest: Fern Canyon with 50-foot walls of ferns; Roosevelt elk herds roam the meadows

name: Torrey Pines State Natural Reserve
interest: Rarest pine tree in the US grows on eroded sandstone bluffs above the Pacific

name: Crystal Cove State Park
interest: 3.2 miles of pristine beach between Laguna Beach and Newport; historic beach cottages

name: Montana de Oro State Park
interest: Mountain of Gold named for golden wildflowers; dramatic bluffs and tide pools near Morro Bay

name: Pfeiffer Big Sur State Park
interest: Heart of Big Sur with redwood groves along the Big Sur River; gateway to Pfeiffer Falls

name: Andrew Molera State Park
interest: Largest state park on the Big Sur coast; seasonal footbridge and pristine beach access

name: Stringfellow Acid Pits
interest: One of California's worst hazardous waste sites; 34M gallons of industrial chemicals dumped 1956-1972

name: Santa Susana Field Laboratory
interest: Former rocket engine test and nuclear reactor site; partial meltdown in 1959; ongoing cleanup disputes

name: Hunters Point Naval Shipyard
interest: Former Navy shipyard with radiological contamination; environmental justice flashpoint amid redevelopment

name: Treasure Island
interest: Artificial island built for 1939 World's Fair; Navy radiological contamination being cleaned for housing

name: McColl Superfund Site
interest: WWII-era petroleum waste sumps in Fullerton; acid sludge disposal caused decades of health complaints

name: Operating Industries Landfill
interest: Monterey Park mega-landfill accepted hazardous waste illegally; one of the largest Superfund sites in CA

name: Aerojet Superfund Site
interest: Rocket propellant manufacturing in Rancho Cordova contaminated 30 sq mi of groundwater with perchlorate and TCE

name: Iron Mountain Mine
interest: Most toxic water ever measured (pH -3.6); acid mine drainage threatens Sacramento River watershed

name: San Fernando Valley Superfund
interest: Massive TCE and PCE groundwater plume affecting drinking water for millions in the LA basin

name: Casmalia Resources Hazardous Waste Site
interest: Former hazardous waste disposal facility near Santa Maria; accepted waste from across California

name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
interest: Nuclear weapons research lab with groundwater VOC plumes; dual Superfund sites (main + Site 300)

name: Camp Pendleton Marine Base
interest: 125,000-acre Marine base with multiple contaminated areas from munitions, fuels, and industrial solvents

name: Montrose Chemical Superfund
interest: Largest DDT manufacturer in US history; massive seafloor contamination off Palos Verdes shelf in Torrance

name: Edwards Air Force Base
interest: Historic flight test center with TCE groundwater contamination beneath the Mojave Desert dry lakebed

name: Selma Treating Superfund Site
interest: Wood treating facility in Selma contaminated soil and groundwater with creosote and PCP

name: Pemaco Superfund Site
interest: Chemical distribution facility in Maywood; VOC contamination in densely populated Latino neighborhood

name: Koppers Co Superfund Site
interest: Former wood treating plant in Oroville; creosote and PCP contamination of soil and groundwater

name: Sacramento Army Depot
interest: Former military logistics depot with TCE groundwater plume; now being redeveloped as industrial park

name: Mather Air Force Base
interest: Closed SAC bomber base with extensive TCE and fuel contamination; converted to civilian airport

name: Concord Naval Weapons Station
interest: 5,000-acre former weapons depot; site of 1944 Port Chicago disaster; massive redevelopment planned

name: Richmond
interest: Chevron refinery town; 2012 refinery fire sent 15,000 to hospitals; major EJ community activism

name: Wilmington
interest: Surrounded by five oil refineries and the Port of LA; one of the most polluted neighborhoods in CA

name: Carson
interest: Home to multiple refineries and the former Cal Compact Landfill; persistent odor complaints

name: Torrance
interest: Site of 2015 ExxonMobil refinery explosion that nearly caused catastrophic release of modified HF acid

name: Martinez
interest: Marathon refinery town on the Carquinez Strait; birthplace of Joe DiMaggio and the martini cocktail

name: El Segundo
interest: Named for Chevron's second Standard Oil refinery; largest refinery on the West Coast

name: Rodeo
interest: Phillips 66 refinery community on San Pablo Bay being converted to world's largest renewable fuels plant

name: Pittsburg
interest: Former steel and chemical manufacturing hub on the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta

name: Antioch
interest: Eastern Contra Costa industrial zone; historically paper mills and chemical plants along the Delta

name: Crockett
interest: Tiny Carquinez Strait town dominated by the C&H Sugar refinery operating since 1906

name: Benicia
interest: Valero refinery town and former state capital; key crude-by-rail terminus on the Carquinez Strait

name: Signal Hill
interest: Tiny city surrounded by Long Beach; 1921 oil gusher made it one of the most productive oil fields ever

name: Vernon
interest: Exclusively industrial city of ~200 residents surrounded by 1,800+ businesses; severe pollution concerns

name: Commerce
interest: Industrial city in southeast LA known for the Citadel Outlets and heavy manufacturing concentration

name: South Gate
interest: Former GM assembly plant city in the industrial corridor of southeast Los Angeles

name: Kettleman City
interest: Tiny farmworker community near CA's largest toxic waste dump; birth defect cluster investigations

name: Mecca
interest: Impoverished farmworker community near the Salton Sea exposed to toxic dust and pesticides

name: Pixley
interest: Small Tulare County community with contaminated drinking water from nitrates and pesticides

name: West Oakland
interest: Diesel particulate hotspot surrounded by port, freeways, and rail; historic Black community

name: Boyle Heights
interest: Historic East LA neighborhood surrounded by freeways; lead contamination from Exide battery recycler

name: Bayview-Hunters Point
interest: SF's last major Black neighborhood adjacent to the contaminated naval shipyard; severe health disparities

name: East Los Angeles
interest: Unincorporated Latino community with disproportionate pollution from industrial and transportation sources

name: Coachella
interest: Farmworker community facing pesticide exposure and Salton Sea air quality issues; also famous for music festival

name: Arvin
interest: Southern San Joaquin Valley town with some of the worst air quality in the US from agriculture and oil

name: Lamont
interest: Unincorporated farmworker community near Bakersfield with pesticide exposure and contaminated water

name: Parlier
interest: Small Fresno County farmworker town with nitrate-contaminated drinking water

name: Shafter
interest: Agricultural community near Bakersfield facing pesticide drift and contaminated groundwater

name: Wasco
interest: Rose-growing capital of the US; farmworker community with environmental health concerns

name: Calipatria
interest: Imperial Valley town near the Salton Sea; claims the world's tallest flagpole at 184 feet below sea level

name: Westmorland
interest: Small Imperial Valley community facing Salton Sea dust exposure and agricultural pollution

name: Huron
interest: One of the poorest cities in CA; farmworker community with severe water contamination issues

name: Avenal
interest: Central Valley prison town near Kettleman City; disproportionate pollution burden on low-income residents

name: McFarland
interest: Subject of the film McFarland, USA; cancer cluster investigation due to pesticide exposure

name: Delano
interest: Birthplace of the 1965 Grape Strike and UFW movement led by Cesar Chavez and Larry Itliong

name: Guadalupe
interest: Small farmworker town near Santa Maria; Guadalupe-Nipomo Dunes complex and buried Cecil B. DeMille set

name: UC Berkeley
interest: Flagship UC campus and top public university; birthplace of the Free Speech Movement

name: Stanford University
interest: Elite research university that seeded Silicon Valley; 8,180-acre campus with Hoover Tower

name: UCLA
interest: Most applied-to university in the US; powerhouse athletics and film school in Westwood

name: UC San Diego
interest: Leading research university in La Jolla; Scripps Institution of Oceanography and biotech corridor

name: Cal Poly San Luis Obispo
interest: Learn by doing polytechnic on the Central Coast; top-ranked CSU with strong engineering and agriculture

name: UC Davis
interest: Premier agricultural and veterinary research university; most bike-friendly campus in the US

name: UC Santa Barbara
interest: Beachfront campus on a Pacific bluff; Nobel Prize-winning physics department

name: UC Irvine
interest: Master-planned campus with circular design; strong computer science and medical programs

name: UC Santa Cruz
interest: Redwood forest campus overlooking Monterey Bay; Banana Slug mascot; genomics research leader

name: San Francisco State University
interest: Birthplace of ethnic studies programs after the 1968 student strike; diverse urban campus

name: San Jose State University
interest: Oldest public university on the West Coast (1857); major Silicon Valley talent pipeline

name: CSU Long Beach
interest: Largest CSU campus by enrollment; Walter Pyramid landmark; strong engineering programs

name: Caltech
interest: Elite STEM institution in Pasadena managing JPL; 40 Nobel laureates affiliated

name: USC
interest: Private research university in downtown LA; top film school and Trojan athletics legacy

name: Cal Poly Pomona
interest: Polytechnic campus with working farm and Arabian horse program on former Kellogg Ranch

name: Golden Gate Bridge
interest: Iconic 1937 Art Deco suspension bridge spanning 4,200 feet across the Golden Gate strait

name: Hollywood Sign
interest: 45-foot-tall letters on Mt Lee originally erected in 1923 as a real estate advertisement

name: Santa Monica Pier
interest: Western terminus of Route 66; iconic Pacific Park Ferris wheel and solar-powered amusement rides

name: Venice Beach
interest: Birthplace of West Coast counterculture; Muscle Beach, skate park, and the Venice Boardwalk

name: Surfrider Beach (Malibu)
interest: World-famous surf break at Malibu Point; birthplace of modern California surf culture

name: Huntington Beach Pier
interest: 1,850-foot concrete pier hosting the annual US Open of Surfing since 1959

name: La Jolla Cove
interest: Pristine marine reserve with sea caves, sea lions, and crystal-clear snorkeling waters

name: Coronado Beach
interest: Consistently ranked top US beach; Hotel del Coronado is a National Historic Landmark since 1888

name: Stinson Beach
interest: Marin County beach town with 3.5 miles of white sand at the foot of Mt Tamalpais

name: Glass Beach (Fort Bragg)
interest: Former dump site where decades of wave action polished discarded glass into smooth colorful pebbles

name: Pfeiffer Beach
interest: Purple-hued sand and Keyhole Arch rock formation; one of Big Sur's most photographed spots

name: Natural Bridges State Beach
interest: Natural sandstone bridge over the surf in Santa Cruz; monarch butterfly wintering site

name: Pismo Beach
interest: Classic California beach town; Oceano Dunes allow vehicles on the beach; famous clam chowder

name: Balboa Pier
interest: Historic 1906 pier on the Balboa Peninsula; Ruby's Diner at the end; fun zone and ferry to Balboa Island

name: Manhattan Beach
interest: Birthplace of beach volleyball; upscale beach community with a classic California pier

name: Napa
interest: Heart of America's premier wine region; over 400 wineries producing world-class Cabernet Sauvignon

name: Sonoma
interest: Where California's wine industry began and the Bear Flag Revolt declared the California Republic

name: Paso Robles
interest: Fast-growing wine region with 200+ wineries; known for bold Zinfandel and Rhone-style blends

name: Salinas
interest: Salad Bowl of the World producing most of America's lettuce; John Steinbeck's hometown

name: Lodi
interest: Home to some of the oldest Zinfandel vines in California; 85+ wineries in the appellation

name: Temecula
interest: Southern California's premier wine region with 40+ wineries in the Temecula Valley AVA

name: Livermore
interest: One of California's oldest wine regions; also home to two national laboratories

name: Santa Ynez
interest: Picturesque Santa Ynez Valley wine country; featured in the film Sideways

name: Boonville (Anderson Valley)
interest: Remote Mendocino County valley known for Pinot Noir, Alsatian varieties, and its own folk language Boontling

name: Healdsburg
interest: Upscale wine country town at the intersection of three premier AVAs: Alexander, Dry Creek, and Russian River Valley

name: St Helena
interest: Heart of Napa Valley with the Culinary Institute of America at Greystone and iconic wineries

name: Calistoga
interest: Hot springs and mud baths at the north end of Napa Valley; home to the Old Faithful geyser of California

name: Los Olivos
interest: Charming tasting room village in Santa Barbara wine country with 30+ tasting rooms on one main street

name: Gonzales
interest: Salinas Valley agricultural town processing lettuce, broccoli, and wine grapes

name: King City
interest: Southern gateway to the Salinas Valley agricultural corridor; gateway to Pinnacles National Park

name: Monterey
interest: Former sardine capital immortalized by Steinbeck; world-class aquarium on Cannery Row

name: Carmel-by-the-Sea
interest: Storybook village with no street addresses; Clint Eastwood was once mayor; gateway to Point Lobos

name: Santa Cruz
interest: Eclectic surf city with the 1924 Giant Dipper roller coaster and redwood-forested UC campus

name: Half Moon Bay
interest: Pumpkin capital of the world; Mavericks big wave surf break draws elite surfers

name: Mendocino
interest: Victorian village on dramatic headlands; stood in for Cabot Cove in Murder She Wrote

name: Eureka
interest: Largest city on the North Coast; ornate Old Town Victorian buildings and working waterfront

name: Bodega Bay
interest: Filming location of Hitchcock's The Birds; prime whale watching and Dungeness crab

name: Cayucos
interest: Laid-back surf town with an 1875 pier; known as the last real California beach town

name: Morro Bay
interest: Dominated by 576-foot volcanic Morro Rock; one of the Nine Sisters chain of volcanic peaks

name: Cambria
interest: Artists' colony near Hearst Castle with a Monterey pine forest and moonstone beach

name: Aptos
interest: Home to the concrete ship SS Palo Alto at Seacliff State Beach; redwood-shaded community

name: Capitola
interest: Oldest seaside resort town on the Pacific Coast; colorful Venetian-style beachfront buildings

name: Pacific Grove
interest: Butterfly Town USA where monarchs overwinter; beautiful rocky coastline and Victorian homes

name: Sausalito
interest: Charming waterfront town across the Golden Gate from SF; historic houseboat community

name: Tiburon
interest: Affluent Marin peninsula with panoramic SF skyline views and ferry access to Angel Island

name: Beverly Hills
interest: Synonymous with luxury and celebrity; Rodeo Drive shopping district and palatial estates

name: Compton
interest: Birthplace of West Coast hip-hop; historically underserved community now experiencing revitalization

name: Palo Alto
interest: Heart of Silicon Valley adjacent to Stanford; HP garage is the birthplace of the tech industry

name: East Palo Alto
interest: Once had the highest per-capita murder rate in the US; now facing severe gentrification pressure

name: Atherton
interest: Most expensive zip code in America; average home price over $7 million on tree-lined estates

name: East Oakland
interest: Historically disinvested community with food deserts and pollution burden; strong community organizing

name: Bel Air
interest: Ultra-exclusive LA enclave with mega-mansions; part of the Platinum Triangle with Beverly Hills and Holmby Hills

name: South Central Los Angeles
interest: Historic Black and Latino community; site of 1965 Watts and 1992 LA uprisings; ongoing environmental justice struggles

name: San Marino
interest: Home to The Huntington Library and one of the wealthiest small cities in California

name: Pomona
interest: Historic Inland Empire city with LA County Fair and growing arts district amid economic challenges

name: Hillsborough
interest: Exclusively residential town with no commercial districts; median home value over $5 million

name: Daly City
interest: Most densely populated city in San Mateo County; Malvina Reynolds' Little Boxes inspired by its tract homes

name: Cupertino
interest: Home to Apple Park spaceship campus; top-rated schools drive extremely high housing costs

name: Palm Springs
interest: Mid-century modern architecture mecca; LGBTQ+ resort destination; aerial tramway to 8,500 ft

name: Mount Shasta
interest: 14,179-foot stratovolcano sacred to Native peoples; spiritual and New Age pilgrimage destination

name: Mammoth Lakes
interest: Premier California ski resort at 11,053 ft; Long Valley caldera indicates active volcanic system

name: South Lake Tahoe
interest: Largest alpine lake in North America; legendary water clarity and year-round recreation

name: Avalon (Catalina Island)
interest: Only incorporated city on Santa Catalina Island; 1929 Casino building and crystal-clear diving waters

name: Solvang
interest: Danish-themed village founded in 1911 by Danish immigrants; windmills and aebleskiver

name: Ojai
interest: Artist colony in a mountain valley; famous pink sunset moment known as the Pink Moment

name: Idyllwild
interest: Mountain arts community at 5,400 ft in the San Jacinto Mountains; gateway to wilderness

name: Julian
interest: 1870s gold mining town famous for apple pies; charming mountain escape east of San Diego

name: Nevada City
interest: Best-preserved Gold Rush town in California; vibrant arts community and Victorian downtown

name: Grass Valley
interest: Home to the Empire Mine, one of the oldest and richest gold mines in California history

name: Truckee
interest: Historic railroad town near Lake Tahoe and Donner Pass; year-round mountain recreation hub

name: Bishop
interest: Gateway to the Eastern Sierra; Volcanic Tablelands petroglyphs and world-class bouldering

name: Lone Pine
interest: Gateway to Mt Whitney (14,505 ft, highest peak in contiguous US); Alabama Hills film location

name: Bridgeport
interest: Remote Mono County seat; gateway to Bodie ghost town and twin lakes backcountry

name: Tuolumne Meadows
interest: Largest sub-alpine meadow in the Sierra Nevada at 8,600 ft; Yosemite high country gem

name: June Lake
interest: The Switzerland of California on the June Lake Loop; stunning fall colors and ski area

name: Lee Vining
interest: Gateway to Mono Lake with its otherworldly tufa towers; eastern entrance to Yosemite via Tioga Pass

name: Dunsmuir
interest: Historic railroad town on the Sacramento River; surrounded by Castle Crags and Mt Shasta

name: Weed
interest: Small lumber town at the base of Mt Shasta; named for founder Abner Weed, not the plant
